notes: This is the fixed two-dataset canonical-backbone shortlist pilot, not the formal D4RL-9 benchmark.
The original ExpRank_MR passthrough is supplied by the run spec, so six injected controls plus one passthrough produce seven methods per dataset-seed cell.
canonical_signed with negative_scale=1.0 is reported as global_neg_0p11 because its effective constant negative coefficient is canonical_alpha 0.11.
global with negative_scale=0.1 is reported as global_neg_0p011 because its effective constant negative coefficient is 0.11 x 0.1 = 0.011.
The global_neg_0p011 branch is a maximum-coefficient-matched global anchor, not a batch-gradient-budget-matched control.
All three distance tapers use negative_scale=0.1 and therefore have maximum negative coefficient 0.011 before distance decay.
The two D4RL-v2 HDF5 files are pinned by SHA-256; a same-named but byte-different dataset is rejected.
This update reuses the existing canonical two-dataset adapter coefficients and reference distance without new D4RL-specific retuning.
All methods use the same 1M-step horizon, four paired seeds, evaluation cadence, and canonical actor-critic trainer.
No task-score threshold or stationarity tolerance is registered, so terminal slopes are diagnostic and the fixed-horizon classification remains inconclusive.
